dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've made a terrible mistake and people are going to die if I don't fix it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I led an expedition {location_hint} last week to chart a shortcut through the mountains. Against every warning, I pushed the group past the safe markers. We stumbled into a {enemy_type} breeding ground and barely escaped. But we didn't just run: we disturbed their nests, and now they're swarming toward {location} in numbers nobody has seen before. {target_npc} is organizing defenses, but we need {quest_item} to reinforce the barricades and we need it yesterday.
dialogueChunks.outro: This is my fault. Every bit of it. But the people in {location} shouldn't pay for my arrogance. Help me make this right.

playerResponses.text: Blame can wait. Tell me what you need and where to find it.
You stirred up a hornet's nest and now the town pays. I'll help, but you owe them an explanation.
You made this mess. Clean it up yourself.

dialogueChunks.intro: I don't deserve your help, {player_name}. But the people who are suffering because of me do.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I mixed an experimental compound without proper testing: thought I could speed up crop growth for {location}. Instead, it poisoned the soil. The harvest is ruined, and now the contamination is spreading toward the water table. {target_npc} warned me not to use untested formulas, and I didn't listen. The only thing that can neutralize the compound is {quest_item}, found deep in the wetlands {location_hint}. The {enemy_type} have claimed that territory, but it's the only source.
dialogueChunks.outro: A season's harvest, gone because I was too impatient. Please, help me undo this before the damage becomes permanent.

playerResponses.text: Poisoned soil is a crisis worth fixing. Point me to the wetlands.
You owe {target_npc} an apology. But I'll get your remedy.
Your experiment, your consequences. Figure it out.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need to tell you something before you hear it from someone else. I'm the reason {location} is in danger.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I found an old sealed chamber {location_hint} during my travels and opened it, thinking it was an abandoned storehouse. It wasn't. Whatever was locked inside is now loose: the {enemy_type} near {location} have become frenzied since that night, attacking in patterns they never used before. {target_npc} says something is driving them, coordinating them. I think I unleashed it. The only clue I have is the {quest_item} I found scattered around the chamber entrance. If the alchemist can analyze it, we might learn what we're dealing with.
dialogueChunks.outro: I should have left that seal alone. Now I need to help fix what I broke, and I can't do it alone.

playerResponses.text: Unsealed horrors and frenzied monsters. I'll get the samples and we'll figure this out.
You opened a sealed chamber without knowing what was inside? Reckless. But I'll help.
You unleashed something ancient and expect me to clean it up? No.

dialogueChunks.intro: I know that look, {player_name}. Everyone in {location} is giving me the same one. I deserve it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I gambled with the settlement's entire reserve of {quest_item} on a trade deal that turned out to be a scam. A merchant promised triple returns and I believed every word. Now the supplies are gone, the merchant has vanished {location_hint}, and {target_npc} is trying to figure out how to keep people fed with nothing. The fake merchant was last seen heading toward {enemy_type} territory: probably has contacts among them, which should have been my first warning.
dialogueChunks.outro: I was greedy and stupid, and the whole settlement is paying for it. If there's any chance of recovering those supplies, I have to try.

playerResponses.text: A con artist preying on settlements? I'll track them down.
You gambled everything on a stranger's promise. Unbelievable. But the settlement shouldn't starve for your mistake.
You got swindled. That's a lesson, not my problem.
